statement: When nc(B)=1 for all B in [1,256], itl(B) is exactly affine in B with slope delta=(w_prefill(1)+w_decode)/(1+m), and the ITL-binding M can be computed in closed form: M_ITL=clamp(floor(1+(targetITL-itl(1))/delta),1,256), matching a brute scan with zero discrepancy.
regime: nc=1 throughout [1,256]; dev set (MaxNumTokens=8192 not exceeded by any batch)
mechanism: Under nc=1, delta(B) is constant (formulas.py:72-76), making itl(B)=itl(1)+(B-1)*delta exactly affine. The closed-form inversion gives the exact integer crossing. Verified: boundary abs_err=0.0 on 4 of 6 scenarios, machine-epsilon (2.84e-14, 7.11e-15) on 2 — all < 1e-6.
applicability_bounds: Requires nc(B)=1 for all B in the search range. Breaks when MaxBatchSize*AvgOutputTokens exceeds MaxNumTokens (8192) — then delta becomes piecewise and a single-slope inversion mis-estimates the crossing (RP-4). Not validated on benchmark grid (iter-5).

statement: M_ITL is a lower bound on the oracle plateau-onset M*, not M* itself. Realized AvgITL averages over occupancy <=B, so AvgITL<=itl(B); the ITL SLO can be satisfied at batch sizes above M_ITL. The gap (M_truth/M_ITL approx 1.6-2.8 on ITL-binding scenarios) must be closed by an occupancy-gap correction derived from the queue solver.
regime: ITL-binding scenarios (itl_binds=true); gap magnitude requires oracle (iter-3)
mechanism: itl(B) computes the per-iteration time at a full batch of size B (worst-case). Actual AvgITL = integral of per-iter time weighted by occupancy distribution <= B, so the realized average is below itl(B). The ITL SLO is met for larger M than M_ITL predicts.
applicability_bounds: Applies to all ITL-binding scenarios. In iter-2 M_ITL is used as the lower-bound point estimate for itl-or-crossover M-hat; the correction toward true M* requires the queue solver (iter-3).

statement: Saturation throughput S(B)=1000*B/tau(B) is monotonically increasing and concave in B under nc=1, approaching the analytic ceiling S_inf=1000/((1+m)*delta) from below (ratio 0.75-0.96 at B=256 on dev scenarios). Throughput is non-decreasing in M, so the useful upper bound on M comes from the binding latency constraint, not from saturation.
mechanism: tau(B) grows sub-linearly in B under nc=1 (formulas.py:83-86), so B/tau(B) is monotone increasing. The saturation ceiling follows from the affine limit of tau.
applicability_bounds: nc=1 required. Saturation ceiling formula changes when nc>1. Not validated on benchmark grid.

statement: nc=1 is a NECESSARY condition for the ITL closed form to exactly match the brute scan. When nc(B) jumps within [1,256], delta changes at the jump and itl becomes piecewise-affine; a single-slope inversion from B=1 misestimates the crossing by up to one integer step.
mechanism: num_iterations_per_prefill(B) (formulas.py:44-56) is a step function of B*AvgOutputTokens/MaxNumTokens. When it jumps from 1 to 2, delta doubles, breaking the single-slope assumption.
applicability_bounds: Applies at any nc jump. On dev set nc=1 everywhere. On benchmark grid with larger tokens or batch sizes, nc>1 can occur.

statement: The prefill-only TTFT crossing M_TTFT_prefill=clamp(floor(1+(targetTTFT-ttft_prefill(1))/delta),1,256) is an UPPER bound on the true TTFT-binding M, because full TTFT=ttft_prefill(B)+queue_wait and the queue solver adds a positive wait term. Increasing M raises TTFT above the primitive-only estimate.
mechanism: ttft_prefill(B) (formulas.py:93-96) excludes the queue wait by design (docstring: 'queue wait excluded; added by solver'). Full TTFT = ttft_prefill(B) + queue_wait(B). Since queue_wait >= 0, the true TTFT-binding M* <= M_TPF. This is why M_TPF is an upper bound used as the ttft-only cell's M-hat.
applicability_bounds: Upper-bound direction is always true when queue_wait > 0 (any non-zero load). When queue_wait = 0 (zero load), M_TPF equals true M* exactly. Magnitude of the over-estimate requires oracle measurement (iter-3).

statement: The analytic primitives (itl, ttft_prefill, tau, delta) partition params-space into exactly THREE primitive-decidable cells, not four. The 'itl-only' and 'crossover' regimes are indistinguishable from primitives alone — they share an identical primitive signature (itl_binds=1, ttft_pf_binds=0, M_TPF>M_ITL) — because the deciding factor is the M/M/1 queue-wait term, which is absent from all primitives.
regime: All scenarios where ttft_pf_binds=false and itl_binds=true (itl-or-crossover fused cell)
mechanism: ttft_prefill(B) (formulas.py:93-96) excludes the queue wait. In itl-only (targetTTFT=400, loose), the queue wait never pushes TTFT above target. In alpha-low/crossover (targetTTFT=60, tight), the queue wait at small M pushes TTFT above target. Both have ttft_prefill slack to B=256 (M_TPF=256), so the prefill primitive cannot distinguish them. The split requires calling the oracle (/target) on fused-cell scenarios — reserved for iter-3.
applicability_bounds: Applies wherever targetTTFT is the distinguishing factor but ttft_prefill(256) <= targetTTFT (i.e., M_TPF=256). The cell is decidable once the oracle is available. On the dev set the fused cell contains 4 of 6 scenarios (baseline, itl-only, alpha-low, alpha-high).

statement: The relative ORDER of M_TPF and M_ITL — specifically whether M_TPF < M_ITL — is the load-bearing classification signal for the ttft-only regime. The two binding booleans (itl_binds, ttft_pf_binds) alone are insufficient: the ttft-only scenario binds on both (itl(256)>targetITL AND ttft_prefill(256)>targetTTFT), so a naive 2x2 on booleans misclassifies it as crossover. Only the ordering M_TPF < M_ITL, derived from the RP-2/RP-5 bound directions, correctly identifies TTFT as the first-binding constraint.
regime: Scenarios where both itl_binds=true and ttft_pf_binds=true but with different binding orders
mechanism: M_TPF < M_ITL implies true_TTFT_M <= M_TPF < M_ITL <= true_ITL_M (from RP-2 lower bound and RP-5 upper bound). Therefore TTFT strictly binds before ITL regardless of the unknown queue wait. The ttft-only scenario: M_TPF=70, M_ITL=95 (70 < 95), confirmed. Naive 2x2 misclassifies ttft-only as crossover and alpha-low as itl-only (2 errors vs 0 for signed partition).
applicability_bounds: Requires nc=1 (so M_ITL and M_TPF are uniquely defined by affine inversion). The ttft-only cell boundary is the closed-form condition targetTTFT < ttft_prefill(M_ITL), confirmed exact iff on all 6 dev scenarios.
